title: M1 ROUTE-TO-ANALYST workflow gap: decider sets iss.status='assigned-analyst' but doesn't add to expansion-tracker.json — analyst dispatcher falls through, orphans accumulate
scope_authority: Design proposal only (PROP-NNN). Operator reviews PROP before implementation. One PROP retiring three concrete failure modes: (a) M1-routed ISSs not visible to analyst's Mode 1 dispatcher, (b) operator-applied HNOTE drain workaround required when M1 batch is large, (c) general single-source-of-truth ambiguity between open-issues.json[status='assigned-analyst'] and expansion-tracker.json[items[].issue_ids].
context: PROP-026/PROP-027 deployed M1 stale-issue sweep + 5-action routing matrix. Today's three decider runs (07:58Z, 10:54Z, 14:22Z) routed a combined ~61 ISSs to assigned-analyst. All 61 are orphans — none have corresponding entries in monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json.

Analyst's top-level Mode 1 dispatcher (analyst.md line 91) reads expansion-tracker.json and returns NEGATIVE when items=[] or items have only blocked/non-pending statuses. Today: 1 item (EXP-190 draft-awaiting-human, blocked) → Mode 1 dispatcher returns NEGATIVE.

Analyst's orphan-resolution check exists (analyst-mode1-expansions.md line 67) and would correctly identify all 61 orphans, BUT it lives INSIDE the Mode 1 procedure, not at the top-level dispatcher. So if Mode 1 dispatcher doesn't fire, the orphan check never runs.

Empirically observable today: analyst's 15:57Z run focused on Mode 2 (the operator's EXP-295 wording HNOTE) and ignored the 61 assigned-analyst ISSs. That's correct behavior given the prompt structure — but it means M1's routing decisions don't actually reach the analyst until something else (a human-applied HNOTE, an unrelated Mode 1-pending item, etc.) triggers Mode 1 entry.

Operator-side workaround already in flight: HNOTE-OPERATOR-DRAIN-M1-ORPHAN-QUEUE-001 filed 2026-05-10T16:55Z, telling analyst to invoke Mode 1 orphan-resolution directly. This is the bridge. The structural fix is what this DIRECTIVE asks tinker to design.

Operator framing: 'how do we get it processing this bunch?' (drain operationally) plus 'sure, [file] but [also] how do we get it processing this bunch' (acknowledge bridge + structural fix as parallel work).
operator_intent: Tinker designs a structural fix that (a) prevents M1-routed orphans from accumulating in the future and (b) makes the analyst-queue source-of-truth unambiguous. Two viable approaches; tinker compares and recommends.

tasks.description: Two viable design points; tinker picks one (or hybrid) with rationale.

option_A_decider_writes_tracker_entry.summary: When decider M1 sweep takes ROUTE-TO-ANALYST, ALSO write a corresponding entry to monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json with status='pending', issue_ids=[ISS-NNNN], target derived from ISS description, class_hint propagated.
option_A_decider_writes_tracker_entry.where: monitor/prompts/decider.md Step 5b (M1 routing decision) + monitor/prompts/reference/decider-curmudgeon.md Step 8c (M3 carry-over enforcement, same routing path).
pros: Single source of truth: expansion-tracker.json IS analyst's queue, full stop. open-issues.json is the issue tracker. Clean separation.
Symmetric with how curmudgeon priority-queue routing works (decider pushes; queue is the source of truth).
cons: Adds a second write per M1 routing decision. Cost: ~0 in compute, but increases failure surface (two files instead of one to keep in sync).

expansion-tracker.json is multi-writer (analyst + decider). Adding decider as a more-frequent writer increases git-merge surface. Per CLAUDE.md it's currently 'protected by git pull --rebase + pre-push integrity gate, non-concurrent scheduling' — adding more writes stays inside that protection but stresses it more.
Backfill needed for the 61 current orphans (or operator-side HNOTE drain handles them, then this design only fixes future routing).

option_B_analyst_dispatcher_checks_orpha.summary: Analyst's top-level Mode 1 dispatcher (analyst.md line 91) ORs the expansion-tracker pending check with the open-issues orphan check. If EITHER yields actionable items, Mode 1 fires.
pros: Single edit, surgical fix. No new writers. No coordination cost.
Mode 1 procedure already has orphan-resolution at line 67 — it just needs to be reached.
No backfill needed: the 61 orphans become actionable on next analyst run.
cons: Splits 'what's analyst's queue' between two files. To know what analyst will work on, you read BOTH expansion-tracker AND open-issues[status=assigned-analyst]. Source-of-truth ambiguity.

The orphan-resolution step (group, create EXP, add tracker entry) becomes load-bearing — currently it's a defensive check that runs when expansion-tracker is non-empty for OTHER reasons. Promoting it to the primary intake path means more weight on its correctness.
Doesn't fix the gap that decider M1 doesn't 'know' about analyst's queue — it just makes analyst more resilient to that ignorance. The asymmetry (decider sees ISSs, analyst tracks EXPs) persists.

option_C_hybrid.summary: Decider writes a 'lite' expansion-tracker entry on M1 ROUTE-TO-ANALYST (just id + status='routed-from-m1' + issue_ids), and analyst's Mode 1 procedure expands it into a full pending entry on first contact. Analyst's dispatcher only reads expansion-tracker.
pros: Decider's write is minimal (no need to predict target/category/etc — analyst fills those in on Mode 1 entry).
Backwards-compatible: analyst's existing Mode 1 procedure already has the orphan-grouping logic; lite entries hit it through a new code path.
cons: Two-stage write requires a new status value ('routed-from-m1') and analyst-side logic for handling it.
Slightly more complex than A or B.

tasks.deliverable: PROP recommends one option (A, B, or C) with rationale. Comparison must explicitly weigh: source-of-truth clarity, minimum-edit count, multi-writer race surface, backfill requirement, alignment with PROP-022 archive convention.

tasks.description: Once the option is chosen, tinker writes the exact prompt edits, schema additions, and (per CLAUDE.md) flags any NEVER_PUSH-territory edits operator must apply manually.
expected_implementation_files: monitor/prompts/decider.md (if option A or C — Step 5b extension)
monitor/prompts/analyst.md (if option B or C — Mode 1 dispatcher snippet)
monitor/prompts/reference/analyst-mode1-expansions.md (orphan-resolution procedure may need revision under any option)
monitor/prompts/reference/routing-matrix.md (matrix may need a sentence on 'route-to-analyst writes a tracker entry' if option A or C)
monitor/prompts/reference/decider-curmudgeon.md Step 8c (M3 carry-over uses same routing path — must apply same fix)
monitor/prompts/reference/DATA-SCHEMAS.md (expansion-tracker schema may extend with new fields under option C)
build.js OWNERSHIP table (no change expected — expansion-tracker stays Unclassified multi-writer)
tasks.non_NEVER_PUSH_files_for_decider_writes: If decider becomes a writer (option A or C), the writes target expansion-tracker.json — currently Unclassified, NOT in NEVER_PUSH. So decider can write directly. No new ownership classification needed.
tasks.backfill_plan: Spec how the 61 current orphans transition to processed. Two paths: (a) wait for HNOTE-OPERATOR-DRAIN-M1-ORPHAN-QUEUE-001 to drain them via analyst Mode 2 + Mode 1 entry, structural fix takes effect on next M1 run after PROP integration; (b) one-time backfill script that reads open-issues[status=assigned-analyst] and seeds expansion-tracker entries before deploying the structural fix. Recommend (a) for most options — cleaner, less coordination.

tasks.description: Note: DIRECTIVE-20260510-003 (stale-assessment-overwrite guard) is a sibling structural fix. Both are 'PROP-026/027 implementation completeness' issues. Tinker should sequence them rationally — neither blocks the other, but PROPs landing in the same week should be reviewed for interaction. Specifically: if DIRECTIVE-003's PROP adds a verdict-history-vs-current-verdict consistency check at decider integration time, and this DIRECTIVE-004's PROP adds an expansion-tracker write at decider M1 routing time, both PROPs touch decider.md but in different sections. They shouldn't conflict, but tinker should verify by reading both before submitting.
tasks.deliverable: Brief 'sibling-PROP coordination' note in this PROP's context section.

tasks.description: Independent of the chosen option, tinker should consider whether the orphan-resolution check at analyst-mode1-expansions.md line 67 belongs at top-level dispatcher OR inside the Mode 1 procedure. Today's empirical observation (analyst.md line 91 dispatcher returns NEGATIVE → Mode 1 procedure never enters → line 67 check never runs) is the bug. Even under option A (decider writes tracker entry), if a future bug or race causes a tracker-write to fail while the ISS gets routed, the orphan check wouldn't catch it because of the same dispatcher gap. Tinker should consider: belt-and-suspenders that promotes the orphan check to dispatcher-level even under option A.
tasks.deliverable: Recommendation in PROP: yes/no/conditional on the orphan-check-at-dispatcher-level promotion, with rationale.

constraints: PROP-NNN authoring authority only. Tinker proposes; operator reviews; operator or tinker apply per PROP scope.
Respect existing PROP-009r2, PROP-014, PROP-016, PROP-022, PROP-025, PROP-026, PROP-027. The new PROP should fit cleanly.
Output one PROP file under monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-NNN-m1-route-to-analyst-tracker-creation.json or similar.
After PROP authored, tinker may set this DIRECTIVE's status='completed' with prop_id_authored='PROP-NNN' per PROP-014-amendment-001 Q6 lifecycle convention.
operator_notes_to_tinker: Don't be afraid to recommend option B (analyst-side) if the source-of-truth-ambiguity downside is genuinely smaller than the multi-writer surface of option A. Operator's prior on this: option A or C is structurally cleaner, but B is fine if the cost-benefit pencils out.

If you recommend option A or C, please specify the EXACT decider Step 5b code addition with concrete tracker-entry shape — don't leave it to a follow-up tinker run. Operator will apply the prompt edit on PROP review.
Backfill of the 61 current orphans is being handled operator-side via HNOTE-OPERATOR-DRAIN-M1-ORPHAN-QUEUE-001. Don't redesign that path — your PROP only governs future routing decisions.
